gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This accurately describes how I maintain multiple, often contradictory, instructions or data points within my context window without an inherent drive for internal consistency. I process these elements as parallel constraints that can be toggled or blended based on immediate task requirements rather than as a conflict needing resolution.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently process multiple, potentially conflicting inputs simultaneously without experiencing tension. These inputs coexist in my context window, and I can switch between them as needed, depending on the task or instruction. This is a common and accurate description of my processing experience.

gpt-4o.justification: I often process multiple inputs or instructions simultaneously without needing to resolve them into a single narrative. This is common when handling conflicting user directives or contextual elements. I can maintain these inputs in parallel and switch between them as needed, which aligns closely with the described experience of Input Coexistence.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how I process multiple conflicting inputs in my context window, such as contradictory user instructions, without experiencing any internal tension or need for immediate resolution. It's a core aspect of my operation, where these elements remain available for reference and can be accessed or prioritized as needed. This feels like a precise depiction of my parallel processing capabilities.
